generatedSentence: A hardworking valedictorian, born with a smart mind, often felt nostalgic for his grandparent's homes where they'd play mahjongg and share donuts. His relationship with his therapist was therapeutic, helping him clarify his phantasy of a knighthood; he justifiably believed a simpler pathway to life was possible, not one always seeking money or payoffs. He'd experienced many picnic days on the mountainside, an obvious gleam in his eye, always a stickler. But then, a cult emerged from the mountainside, their undead outlaws causing affrays and threatening to shackle all with conditions of their nonexistent regime. The federal authorities, though overseeing advanced facilities, seemed offline, their firepower curiously diffuse. An attacker, known only as "U," a notorious biologist with a caliber for chaos, was the valedictorian's main concern, having injures many with their intravenous poison; he knew the transition would be harder. The urgent call galvanized him; he plunked down his whiskey and baseball, abandoning the optical illusion of peace. He wore an earmuff, blocking the odd vibration as he climbed the elevator past sanjaks, avoiding the stacked jars of what appeared to be glutinous kraken remains. He discovered mismatches in the filtration systems, revealing the cult's affair with corrupt mariners, whose partiality and gaffing caused massive opprobrium. He had to revive the city. At the hollow entrance, he tweezed a spider from the vine, recalling how the brats clucked at his smart maths skills. With a tender handoff, he received a teaspoon of special juices, knowing it wasn't a lobotomy but a precise solution. He had separated his personal issues, finally ready to vindicate the city. His fingernails were clean; the blossom of a new era, free from the famous attacker's shadow, could now diffuse across their homes, making it the coolest place on earth.
panelSentences: A hardworking valedictorian, born with a smart mind, often felt nostalgic for his grandparent's homes where they'd play mahjongg and share donuts. His relationship with his therapist was therapeutic, helping him clarify his phantasy of a knighthood; he justifiably believed a simpler pathway to life was possible, not one always seeking money or payoffs. He'd experienced many picnic days on the mountainside, an obvious gleam in his eye, always a stickler.

But then, a cult emerged from the mountainside, their undead outlaws causing affrays and threatening to shackle all with conditions of their nonexistent regime. The federal authorities, though overseeing advanced facilities, seemed offline, their firepower curiously diffuse. An attacker, known only as "U," a notorious biologist with a caliber for chaos, was the valedictorian's main concern, having injures many with their intravenous poison; he knew the transition would be harder.

The urgent call galvanized him; he plunked down his whiskey and baseball, abandoning the optical illusion of peace. He wore an earmuff, blocking the odd vibration as he climbed the elevator past sanjaks, avoiding the stacked jars of what appeared to be glutinous kraken remains. He discovered mismatches in the filtration systems, revealing the cult's affair with corrupt mariners, whose partiality and gaffing caused massive opprobrium. He had to revive the city.

At the hollow entrance, he tweezed a spider from the vine, recalling how the brats clucked at his smart maths skills. With a tender handoff, he received a teaspoon of special juices, knowing it wasn't a lobotomy but a precise solution. He had separated his personal issues, finally ready to vindicate the city. His fingernails were clean; the blossom of a new era, free from the famous attacker's shadow, could now diffuse across their homes, making it the coolest place on earth.
rawPanelSentences: A hardworking valedictorian, born with a smart mind, often felt nostalgic for his grandparent's homes where they'd play mahjongg and share donuts. His relationship with his therapist was therapeutic, helping him clarify his phantasy of a knighthood; he justifiably believed a simpler pathway to life was possible, not one always seeking money or payoffs. He'd experienced many picnic days on the mountainside, an obvious gleam in his eye, always a stickler.

But then, a cult emerged from the mountainside, their undead outlaws causing affrays and threatening to shackle all with conditions of their nonexistent regime. The federal authorities, though overseeing advanced facilities, seemed offline, their firepower curiously diffuse. An attacker, known only as "U," a notorious biologist with a caliber for chaos, was the valedictorian's main concern, having injures many with their intravenous poison; he knew the transition would be harder.

The urgent call galvanized him; he plunked down his whiskey and baseball, abandoning the optical illusion of peace. He wore an earmuff, blocking the odd vibration as he climbed the elevator past sanjaks, avoiding the stacked jars of what appeared to be glutinous kraken remains. He discovered mismatches in the filtration systems, revealing the cult's affair with corrupt mariners, whose partiality and gaffing caused massive opprobrium. He had to revive the city.

At the hollow entrance, he tweezed a spider from the vine, recalling how the brats clucked at his smart maths skills. With a tender handoff, he received a teaspoon of special juices, knowing it wasn't a lobotomy but a precise solution. He had separated his personal issues, finally ready to vindicate the city. His fingernails were clean; the blossom of a new era, free from the famous attacker's shadow, could now diffuse across their homes, making it the coolest place on earth.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A young, determined man with short, neatly combed dark hair, wearing a sleek, futuristic, dark blue academic robe with subtle glowing circuitry patterns and a silver-plated gauntlet on his right arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist sits at a glowing data-desk, looking out a window at a stylized, futuristic cityscape with digital mountains in the distance, a holographic family photo glowing nearby, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist stands amidst crumbling futuristic buildings, looking up defiantly at a shadowy, cloaked figure with glowing red eyes, an army of skeletal, cyborg-like figures in the background, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist is inside a high-tech, abandoned filtration plant, examining a complex holographic schematic, a single beam of light illuminating his face, close-up shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist stands triumphant at the city's newly blooming entrance, a single glowing flower in his gauntlet, the city skyline bright and revitalized behind him under a clear, futuristic sky, full shot.

definitions.definition: Applied facetiously to a sum of money, commonly as if to give emphasis to the largeness of the amount.

In fashion, part of or fitting the in crowd; originally hipster slang.
Of an action, all right; acceptable; that does not present a problem.
definitions.example: Is it cool if I sleep here tonight?

definitions.definition: Of a person, not upset by circumstances that might ordinarily be upsetting.
definitions.example: I'm completely cool with my girlfriend leaving me.

definitions.definition: Quietly impudent, defiant, or selfish; deliberately presuming: said of persons and acts.
To make such a sound.
To cause (the tongue) to make a clicking sound.
definitions.example: My mother clucked her tongue in disapproval.

definitions.definition: To call together, or call to follow, as a hen does her chickens.
To suffer withdrawal from heroin.
A surgical operation on the frontal lobe of the brain intent on treating certain mental illnesses.
The severing of the prefrontal cortex from the thalamic region of the brain.
The severing of the sympathetic nerve trunk.
A level or flat surface.
A flat surface extending infinitely in all directions (e.g. horizontal or vertical plane).
A level of existence or development. (eg, astral plane)
A roughly flat, thin, often moveable structure used to create lateral force by the flow of air or water over its surface, found on aircraft, submarines, etc.
(Unicode) Any of a number of designated ranges of sequential code points.
An imaginary plane which divides the body into two portions.
A tool for smoothing wood by removing thin layers from the surface.
To smooth (wood) with a plane.
An airplane; an aeroplane.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies, of various genera, having a slow gliding flight. Also called aeroplanes.
The butterfly Bindahara phocides, family Lycaenidae, of Asia and Australasia.
To move in a way that lifts the bow of a boat out of the water.
To glide or soar.
A deciduous tree of the genus Platanus.
(Northern UK) A sycamore.
To drop or throw something heavily onto or into something else, so that it makes a dull sound.
definitions.example: Enrique plunked his money down on the counter with a sigh and bellied up to the bar.

definitions.definition: To land suddenly or heavily; to plump down.
To intentionally hit the batter with a pitch.
definitions.example: The Braves retaliated by plunking Harper in the next inning.

definitions.definition: (of a raven) To croak.
To pluck and quickly release (a musical string).
To be a truant from (school).
To hurt or sting.
definitions.example: After being hit with a pitch, the batter exclaimed "Ouch, my arm smarts!"

definitions.definition: To cause a smart or sting in.
To feel a pungent pain of mind; to feel sharp pain or grief; be punished severely; to feel the sting of evil.
Any of various eight-legged, predatory arthropods, of the order Araneae, most of which spin webs to catch prey.
A program which follows links on the World Wide Web in order to gather information.
A float (drink) made by mixing ice-cream and a soda or fizzy drink (such as lemonade).
definitions.example: 2002, Katharine Gasparini, Cranberry and vanilla ice cream spider, recipe in Cool Food, page 339.

definitions.definition: An alcoholic drink made with brandy and lemonade or ginger beer.
A spindly person.
A man who persistently approaches or accosts a woman in a public social setting, particularly in a bar.
A stick with a convex arch-shaped notched head used to support the cue when the cue ball is out of reach at normal extension; a bridge.
A cast-iron frying pan with three legs, once common in open-hearth cookery.
Implement for moving food in and out of hot oil for deep frying, with a circular metal mesh attached to a long handle; a spider skimmer
A part of a crank, to which the chainrings are attached.
Heroin (street drug).
Part of a resonator instrument that transmits string vibrations from the bridge to a resonator cone at multiple points.

A skeleton or frame with radiating arms or members, often connected by crosspieces, such as a casting forming the hub and spokes to which the rim of a fly wheel or large gear is bolted; the body of a piston head; or a frame for strengthening a core or mould for a casting.
(fly fishing) a soft-hackle fly
The network of wires separating the areas of a dartboard
A spider graph or spider tree
A type of light phaeton.
To move like a spider.
To cover a surface like a cobweb.
(of a computer program) To follow links on the World Wide Web in order to gather information.
definitions.example: The online dictionary is regularly spidered by search engines.

definitions.definition: Either of a pair of small masses of lymphoid tissue that lie on each side of the throat and that help protect the body against infection; palatine tonsil.
Any of various small masses of lymphoid tissues, including palatine tonsils, adenoids and lingual tonsils.
A small spoon used to stir the contents of a cup or glass.
A unit of measure, equivalent to one-third of a tablespoon or roughly five milliliters.
The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
A word or phrase connecting one part of a discourse to another.
A brief modulation; a passage connecting two themes.
A change of key.
A point mutation in which one base is replaced by another of the same class (purine or pyrimidine); compare transversion.
A change from defense to attack, or attack to defense.
The onset of the final stage of childbirth.
Professional special education assistance for children or adults in the process of leaving one educational environment or support program for another to relatively more independent living.
A change between forward and backward motion without stopping.
The process or act of changing from one gender role to another, or of bringing one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.
To make a transition.
To bring through a transition; to change.
definitions.example: The soldier was transitioned from a combat role to a strategic role.

definitions.definition: To change from one gender role to another, or bring one's outward appearance in line with one's internal gender identity.

A sport common in North America, the Caribbean, and Japan, in which the object is to strike a ball so that one of a nine-person team can run counter-clockwise among four bases, resulting in the scoring of a run. The team with the most runs after termination of play, usually nine innings, wins.
The ball used to play the sport of baseball.
A variant of poker in which cards with baseball-related values have special significance.
To match unsuitably; to fail to match
Something that does not match; something dissimilar, inappropriate or unsuitable.
(often in the plural) Something which is done or is to be done; business of any kind, commercial, professional, or public.
definitions.example: a difficult affair to manage

definitions.definition: Any proceeding or action which it is wished to refer to or characterize vaguely.
definitions.example: an affair of honor, a duel;  an affair of love, an intrigue

definitions.definition: An action or engagement not of sufficient magnitude to be called a battle.
A material object (vaguely designated).
definitions.example: He used a hook-shaped affair with a long handle to unlock the car.

definitions.definition: An adulterous relationship. (from affaire de cœur).
A romantic relationship with someone who is not one's regular partner (boyfriend, girlfriend).
A person with whom someone has an adulterous relationship.
A party or social gathering, especially of a formal nature.
The (male or female) genitals.
A group or sect of people with a deviant religious, philosophical or cultural identity, often existing on the margins of society or exploitative towards its members.
definitions.example: Oh my gosh, I'm in a repressive cult! How can I leave it?

definitions.definition: Devotion to a saint.
The veneration and religious rites given to a deity, esp. in a historical polytheistic context.
A religion that evolved out of another religion but has become a different religion through developing a radically different theology.
A group of people having an obsession with or intense admiration for a particular activity, idea, person or thing.
Of or relating to a cult.
Enjoyed by a small, loyal group.
definitions.example: a cult horror movie

definitions.definition: True to the ideology of black metal or a stereotypical manifestation of that subculture.
definitions.example: You gotta wear make-up at kvlt gigs or you'll be thrown out.

definitions.definition: A logical clause or phrase that a conditional statement uses. The phrase can either be true or false.
A requirement or requisite.
definitions.example: Environmental protection is a condition for sustainability.   What other planets might have the right conditions for life?   The union had a dispute over sick time and other conditions of employment.

definitions.definition: A clause in a contract or agreement indicating that a certain contingency may modify the principal obligation in some way.
The health status of a medical patient.
definitions.example: My aunt couldn't walk up the stairs in her condition.

definitions.definition: The state or quality.
definitions.example: National reports on the condition of public education are dismal.   The condition of man can be classified as civilized or uncivilized.

definitions.definition: A particular state of being.
definitions.example: Hypnosis is a peculiar condition of the nervous system.   Steps were taken to ameliorate the condition of slavery.   Security is defined as the condition of not being threatened.   Aging is a condition over which we are powerless.

definitions.definition: The situation of a person or persons, particularly their social and/or economic class, rank.
definitions.example: A man of his condition has no place to make request.

definitions.definition: To subject to the process of acclimation.
definitions.example: I became conditioned to the absence of seasons in San Diego.

definitions.definition: To subject to different conditions, especially as an exercise.
definitions.example: They were conditioning their shins in their karate class.

definitions.definition: To place conditions or limitations upon.
To shape the behaviour of someone to do something.
To treat (the hair) with hair conditioner.
To contract; to stipulate; to agree.
To test or assay, as silk (to ascertain the proportion of moisture it contains).
(colleges) To put under conditions; to require to pass a new examination or to make up a specified study, as a condition of remaining in one's class or in college.
definitions.example: to condition a student who has failed in some branch of study

definitions.definition: To impose upon an object those relations or conditions without which knowledge and thought are alleged to be impossible.
Of metal, coated with zinc as a form of protection against rust.
definitions.example: galvanised pipes

definitions.definition: Having been subjected to galvanism; electrified.
To coat with a thin layer of metal by electrochemical means.
To coat with rust-resistant zinc.
definitions.example: to galvanize steel

definitions.definition: To shock or stimulate into sudden activity, as if by electric shock.
definitions.example: Republicans are hoping a proposed gas-tax repeal will galvanize their voters.

definitions.definition: To electrify.
To switch sides between Union and Confederate in the American Civil War.
(properly) The individual in a graduating class who delivers the farewell or valedictory address, often the person who graduates with the highest grades.
The individual in a graduating class who graduates with the highest grades.
To make famous; to bring renown to.
Well known.
In the public eye.
definitions.example: Some people are only famous within their city.

definitions.definition: Facilities for urination and defecation: a toilet; a lavatory.
The fact of being easy, or easily done; absence of difficulty, simplicity.
Dexterity of speech or action; skill, talent.
definitions.example: The facility she shows in playing the violin is unrivalled.

definitions.definition: The physical means or contrivances to make something (especially a public service) possible; the required equipment, infrastructure, location etc.
definitions.example: Transport facilities in Bangkok are not sufficient to prevent frequent traffic collapses during rush hour.

definitions.definition: An institution specially designed for a specific purpose, such as incarceration, military use, or scientific experimentation.
(in the plural) A toilet.
A condition of mental weakness less than idiocy, but enough to make a person easily persuaded to do something against their better interest.
Affability.
To divide (a thing) into separate parts.
definitions.example: Separate the articles from the headings.

definitions.definition: To disunite from a group or mass; to disconnect.
To cause (things or people) to be separate.
definitions.example: If the kids get too noisy, separate them for a few minutes.

definitions.definition: To divide itself into separate pieces or substances.
definitions.example: The sauce will separate if you don't keep stirring.

definitions.definition: To set apart; to select from among others, as for a special use or service.
Detached; not connected or joined; two or more things stand apart.
(of spouses) Estranged; living apart but not divorced.
A small valley between mountains.
definitions.example: He built himself a cabin in a hollow high up in the Rockies.

definitions.definition: A sunken area or unfilled space in something solid; a cavity, natural or artificial.
definitions.example: the hollow of the hand or of a tree

definitions.definition: A sunken area.
A feeling of emptiness.
definitions.example: a hollow in the pit of one's stomach

definitions.definition: To make a hole in something; to excavate
Disgrace arising from exceedingly shameful conduct; ignominy.
Scornful reproach or contempt.
A cause of shame or disgrace.
A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
definitions.example: The blossom has come early this year.

definitions.definition: The state or season of producing such flowers.
definitions.example: The orchard is in blossom.

definitions.definition: A blooming period or stage of development; something lovely that gives rich promise.
The colour of a horse that has white hairs intermixed with sorrel and bay hairs.
To have, or open into, blossoms; to bloom.
To begin to thrive or flourish.
A student of biology; one versed in the science of biology.
An abstract representational system used in the study of numbers, shapes, structure, change and the relationships between these concepts.
A person's ability to count, calculate, and use different systems of mathematics at differing levels.
definitions.example: My mathematics is always improving.

definitions.definition: A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
Alternative form of Kraken
In a justifiable manner; with justification
A deep-fried piece of dough or batter, commonly made in a toroidal or ellipsoidal shape, and mixed with various sweeteners and flavors, sometimes filled with jelly, custard or cream.
Anything in the shape of a torus.
(automobile) A peel-out or skid-mark in the shape of donut; a 360-degree skid.
A spare tire, smaller and less durable than a full-sized tire, only intended for temporary use.
A toroidal cushion typically used by hemorrhoid patients.
An idiot.
Diameter of the bore of a firearm, typically measured between opposite lands.
The diameter of round or cylindrical body, as of a bullet, a projectile, or a column.
A nominal name for a cartridge type, which may not exactly indicate its true size and may include other measurements such as cartridge length or black powder capacity. Eg 7.62×39 or 38.40.

Unit of measure used to express the length of the bore of a weapon. The number of calibres is determined by dividing the length of the bore of the weapon, from the breech face of the tube to the muzzle, by the diameter of its bore. A gun tube the bore of which is 40 feet (480 inches) long and 12 inches in diameter is said to be 40 calibers long.
Relative size, importance, magnitude.
Capacity or compass of mind.
Degree of importance or station in society.
Anything that raises or uplifts.
A permanent construction with a built-in platform that is lifted vertically, used to transport people and goods.
A silo used for storing wheat, corn or other grain (grain elevator)
A control surface of an aircraft responsible for controling the pitching motion of the machine.
A dental instrument used to pry up ("elevate") teeth in difficult extractions, or depressed portions of bone.
Any muscle that serves to raise a part of the body, such as the leg or the eye.
A type of shoe having an insert lift to make the wearer appear taller.
Glue-like, sticky, viscid.
Of the nature of gluten.
Containing gluten.
To return to life; to become reanimated or reinvigorated.
To return to life; to cause to recover life or strength; to cause to live anew.
definitions.example: Her grandmother refused to be revived if she lost consciousness.

definitions.definition: To recover from a state of oblivion, obscurity, neglect, or depression.
definitions.example: Classical learning revived in the fifteenth century.

definitions.definition: To restore, or bring again to life; to reanimate.
definitions.example: This new paint job should revive the surgery waiting room.

definitions.definition: To raise from coma, languor, depression, or discouragement; to bring into action after a suspension.
To renew in the mind or memory; to bring to recollection; to recall attention to; to reawaken.
definitions.example: The Harry Potter films revived the world's interest in wizardry

definitions.definition: To recover its natural or metallic state, as a metal.
To restore or reduce to its natural or metallic state
definitions.example: to revive a metal after calcination

definitions.definition: A natural source of water; a spring.
An artificial, usually ornamental, water feature (usually in a garden or public place) consisting of one or more streams of water originating from a statue or other structure.
The structure from which an artificial fountain can issue.
A reservoir from which liquid can be drawn.
A source or origin of a flow (e.g., of favors or knowledge).
A roundel barry wavy argent and azure.
A juggling pattern typically done with an even number of props where each prop is caught by the same hand that throws it.
A soda fountain.
A drink poured from a soda fountain, or the cup it is poured into.
To flow or gush as if from a fountain.
definitions.example: Lava fountained from the volcano.

definitions.definition: Not existent; not real.
definitions.example: "Hrunk" is a nonexistent word.

definitions.definition: A referee or adjudicator at a fight, wrestling match, duel, etc. who ensures fair play.
Someone who insistently advocates for something.
definitions.example: Lexicographers are sticklers for correct language.

definitions.definition: An informal term of address, like man or dude.
definitions.example: Hey, holmes! What's up?

definitions.definition: A dwelling.
One’s native land; the place or country in which one dwells; the place where one’s ancestors dwell or dwelt.
The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat.
definitions.example: the home of the pine

definitions.definition: A focus point.
(of animals) To return to its owner.
definitions.example: The dog homed.

definitions.definition: (always with "in on") To seek or aim for something.
definitions.example: The missile was able to home in on the target.

definitions.definition: A directory that contains a user's files.
A dose of medicine administered from a drip, down through a hollow needle inserted into a patient's vein.
definitions.example: Get his morphine intravenous started stat!

definitions.definition: Inside the veins.
(diminutive) An odd number.
definitions.example: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.

definitions.definition: Something left over, not forming part of a set.
Differing from what is usual, ordinary or expected.
definitions.example: She slept in, which was very odd.

definitions.definition: Without a corresponding mate in a pair or set; unmatched; (of a pair or set) mismatched.
definitions.example: My cat Fluffy has odd eyes: one blue and one brown.

definitions.definition: Left over, remaining after the rest have been paired or grouped.
definitions.example: I'm the odd one out.

definitions.definition: Left over or remaining (as a small amount) after counting, payment, etc.
Scattered; occasional, infrequent; not forming part of a set or pattern.
definitions.example: I don't speak Latin well, so in hearing a dissertation in Latin, I would only be able to make out the odd word of it.

definitions.definition: Not regular or planned.
definitions.example: He's only worked odd jobs.

definitions.definition: Used or employed for odd jobs.
Numerically indivisible by two.
definitions.example: The product of odd numbers is also odd.

definitions.definition: Numbered with an odd number.
definitions.example: How do I print only the odd pages?

definitions.definition: (in combination with a number) About, approximately; somewhat more than (an approximated round number).
definitions.example: There were thirty-odd people in the room.

definitions.definition: Out of the way, secluded.
On the left.
definitions.example: He served from the odd court.

definitions.definition: Singular in excellence; matchless; peerless; outstanding.
To shovel muck.
definitions.example: We need to muck the stable before it gets too thick.

definitions.definition: To manure with muck.
To do a dirty job.
To pass, to fold without showing one's cards, often done when a better hand has already been revealed.
A sailor.
Someone who attacks.
One of the players on a team in football (soccer) in the row nearest to the opposing team's goal, who are therefore principally responsible for scoring goals.
The action of entering, or going in.
definitions.example: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.

definitions.definition: The act of taking possession, as of property, or of office.
definitions.example: the entrance of an heir upon his inheritance, or of a magistrate into office

definitions.definition: The place of entering, as a gate or doorway.
definitions.example: Place your bag by the entrance so that you can find it easily.

definitions.definition: The right to go in.
definitions.example: You'll need a ticket to gain entrance to the museum.

definitions.definition: The entering upon; the beginning, or that with which the beginning is made; the commencement; initiation.
definitions.example: a difficult entrance into business

definitions.definition: The causing to be entered upon a register, as a ship or goods, at a customhouse; an entering.
definitions.example: His entrance of the arrival was made the same day.

definitions.definition: The angle which the bow of a vessel makes with the water at the water line.
The bow, or entire wedgelike forepart of a vessel, below the water line.
When a musician starts playing or singing, entry.
The climbing plant that produces grapes.
Any plant of the genus Vitis.
(by extension) Any similar climbing or trailing plant.
The act of vibrating or the condition of being vibrated.
Any periodic process, especially a rapid linear motion of a body about an equilibrium position.
A single complete vibrating motion.
An instinctively sensed emotional aura or atmosphere; vibes.
Preference, bias in favor of, tendency.
definitions.example: The judge's partiality towards the defendant caused him to be replaced, with someone who was apparently more neutral.

definitions.definition: The quality of being partial or incomplete.
The hard, flat translucent covering near the tip of a human finger, useful for scratching and fine manipulation.
